codeReview.summary: The feature meets the literal "no structuredClone in transitions" target, but the clone-removal refactor introduced a behavioral regression: selectPlanFeatures mutates the caller's input session object via shared execution references.

issues.description: prepareDraftPlanEdit returns `{ ...session }` without cloning `execution`, and selectPlanFeatures later calls `clearExecution(next)` (plan.ts:304), which mutates `session.execution` in place (shared.ts:73-82). This violates transition purity and changes behavior after replacing deep clone semantics. Repro: `bun -e "...selectPlanFeatures..."` yields `before='before', afterInput=null` for the same input object.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-to-commit traceability is ambiguous for no-op verification sessions: the m4-clone-reduction handoff commit points to m4-constants-util-merge, while the actual structuredClone removal landed earlier.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId is 080edf8 (constants/util merge diff has no structuredClone removal); `git show 24846eb -- src/runtime/transitions/shared.ts` contains the `structuredClone` deletion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The core-worker procedure lacks explicit guidance for already-implemented/no-op features, which leads to `followedProcedure: true` handoffs without test-first evidence or feature-specific code changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: core-worker SKILL.md mandates test-first + implementation commit per feature, but transcript/handoff for workerSessionId 7246eda0-6589-48eb-9d90-859b7793e60b reports no code changes and only validation on existing HEAD.

summary: Reviewed feature m4-clone-reduction using the handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and skill procedure. Found one blocking regression tied to clone-removal semantics: input session mutation in plan transition flow. Report marked fail.
